revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=寺戸大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=京都府向日市 |位置= |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長98m 高さ9m |築造年代=4世紀 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=和製銅鏡・銅鏃
中国製銅鏡 |史跡指定= |}} '''寺戸大塚古墳'''（てらどおおつかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]]の西ノ岡（向日丘陵）に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期の[[前方後円墳]]である。 == 概要 == 向日丘陵古墳群中の1基。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期中葉とみられている。やや西に傾いており、前方、後円ともに[[竪穴式石室]]をもつ。 出土した三角縁神獣鏡が[[椿井大塚山古墳]]のものと[[同氾]]関係にあると見られ、[[椿井大塚山古墳]]の[[被葬者]]の支配下にあった者の墓ではないかと言われている。 現在は竹やぶの土入れにより前方部の大半が失われている。 墳頂には8m四方の埴輪をめぐらせた区画があり、その真下に石室がある。 土師器の供献場がある。 == 規模・形状 == *全長 - 98m *高さ - 9m *前方部の幅 - 47m *後円部の直径 - 57m == 埋葬施設 == 前方部に長さ5.2m、幅1m、高さ1.3mの竪穴式石室をもつ。 後円部に長さ5.6m、幅0.8m、高さ1.6mの竪穴式石室をもつ。 == 副葬品 == *前方部 **浮彫式獣帯鏡 **三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡 **方格規矩四神鏡 **琴柱形石製品 **管玉 **刀剣 **槍 **銅鏃 **鉄鏃 **鉄斧 **鉄鎌 *後円部 **三角縁神獣鏡 **合子 **勾玉 **石釧 **管玉 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿の史跡一覧]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:京都府の史跡]] [[Category:向日市]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

中国製銅鏡 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「寺戸大塚古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |}} {{osm box|n|2144126959}} '''寺戸大塚古墳'''（てらどおおつかこふん）は、[[京都市]][[西京区]]と[[京都府]][[向日市]]の境の西ノ岡（向日丘陵）に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期の[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 向日丘陵古墳群中の1基。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期中葉とみられている。やや西に傾いており、前方、後円ともに[[竪穴式石室]]をもつ。 出土した三角縁神獣鏡が[[椿井大塚山古墳]]のものと[[同氾]]関係にあると見られ、[[椿井大塚山古墳]]の[[被葬者]]の支配下にあった者の墓ではないかと言われている。 現在は竹やぶの土入れにより前方部の大半が失われている。 墳頂には8m四方の埴輪をめぐらせた区画があり、その真下に石室がある。 土師器の供献場がある。 == 規模・形状 == *全長 - 98m *後円部高さ - 18m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf 南孝雄「寺戸大塚古墳 -向日丘陵の古墳時代前期の首長墓-」] *後円部の直径 - 57m == 埋葬施設 == 前方部に長さ 5.3 m、幅約 0.9 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 後円部に長さ 6.45 m、幅約 0.8 m、高さ 1.6 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 == 副葬品 == *前方部 **浮彫式獣帯鏡 **三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡 **方格規矩四神鏡 **琴柱形石製品 **管玉 **刀剣 **槍 **銅鏃 **鉄鏃 **鉄斧 **鉄鎌 *後円部 **三角縁神獣鏡 **合子 **勾玉 **石釧 **管玉 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 寺戸大塚古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 寺戸大塚古墳自体は、平成27年に「寺戸大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 *: 平成28年3月1日、既指定の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「寺戸大塚古墳」の3ヶ所に他の8基を追加指定、「乙訓古墳群」に名称変更平成28年3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。。 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿の史跡一覧]] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てらとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:向日市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

後円部 |所在地=京都府向日市寺戸町芝山 |位置={{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|57|21|N|135|41|19|E|scale:10000}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長98m 高さ9m |築造年代=4世紀 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=和製銅鏡・銅鏃
中国製銅鏡 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]]「寺戸大塚古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |}} '''寺戸大塚古墳'''（てらどおおつかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 向日丘陵古墳群中の1基。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期中葉とみられている。やや西に傾いており、前方、後円ともに[[竪穴式石室]]をもつ。 出土した三角縁神獣鏡が[[椿井大塚山古墳]]のものと[[同氾]]関係にあると見られ、[[椿井大塚山古墳]]の[[被葬者]]の支配下にあった者の墓ではないかと言われている。 現在は竹やぶの土入れにより前方部の大半が失われている。 墳頂には8m四方の埴輪をめぐらせた区画があり、その真下に石室がある。 土師器の供献場がある。 == 規模 == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] *全長 - 98m *後円部高さ - 18m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf 南孝雄「寺戸大塚古墳 -向日丘陵の古墳時代前期の首長墓-」] *後円部直径 - 57m == 埋葬施設 == 前方部に長さ 5.3 m、幅約 0.9 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 後円部に長さ 6.45 m、幅約 0.8 m、高さ 1.6 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 == 副葬品 == *前方部 **浮彫式獣帯鏡 **三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡 **方格規矩四神鏡 **琴柱形石製品 **管玉 **刀剣 **槍 **銅鏃 **鉄鏃 **鉄斧 **鉄鎌 *後円部 **三角縁神獣鏡 **合子 **勾玉 **石釧 **管玉 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 寺戸大塚古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 寺戸大塚古墳自体は、平成27年に「寺戸大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定平成27年3月10日文部科学省告示第38号。 *: 平成28年3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「寺戸大塚古墳」の3件を統合、これに他の8基の古墳を追加指定のうえ、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（文化庁記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} {{osm box|n|2144126959}} *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿の史跡一覧]] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てらとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:向日市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） }} '''寺戸大塚古墳'''（てらどおおつかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 向日丘陵古墳群中の1基。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期中葉とみられている。やや西に傾いており、前方部、後円部ともに[[竪穴式石室]]をもつ。 出土した[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が[[椿井大塚山古墳]]のものと同笵関係（同一の鋳型または原型を使用して鋳造したもの）にあると見られ、[[椿井大塚山古墳]]の[[被葬者]]の支配下にあった者の墓ではないかと言われている。現在は竹やぶの土入れにより前方部の大半が失われている。 墳頂には8[[メートル|m]]四方の[[埴輪]]をめぐらせた区画があり、その真下に石室がある。[[土師器]]の供献場がある。 == 規模 == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] *全長 - 98m *後円部高さ - 18m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf 南孝雄「寺戸大塚古墳 -向日丘陵の古墳時代前期の首長墓-」] *後円部直径 - 57m == 埋葬施設 == 前方部に長さ 5.3 m、幅約 0.9 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 後円部に長さ 6.45 m、幅約 0.8 m、高さ 1.6 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 == 副葬品 == *前方部 **浮彫式獣帯鏡 **三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡 **方格規矩四神鏡 **琴柱形石製品 **[[管玉]] **[[刀剣]] **[[槍]] **銅[[鏃]] **[[鉄鏃]] **鉄[[斧]] **鉄[[鎌]] *後円部 **三角縁神獣鏡 **[[合子]] **[[勾玉]] **石釧 **管玉 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 寺戸大塚古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 寺戸大塚古墳自体は、2015年（[[平成]]27年）に「寺戸大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定平成27年（2015年）3月10日[[文部科学省]][[告示]]第38号。 *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「寺戸大塚古墳」の3件を統合、これに他の8基の古墳を追加指定のうえ、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年（2016年）3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（[[文化庁]]記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} {{osm box|n|2144126959}} *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿地方の史跡一覧]] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:てらとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:向日市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=寺戸大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
後円部 |所在地=[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |位置={{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=寺戸大塚
古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長98[[メートル|m]] 高さ9m |築造年代=4世紀 |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者= |出土品=和製[[銅鏡]]・銅[[鏃]]
中国製銅鏡 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]]「寺戸大塚古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） }} '''寺戸大塚古墳'''（てらどおおつかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 向日丘陵古墳群中の1基。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期中葉とみられている。やや西に傾いており、前方部、後円部ともに[[竪穴式石室]]をもつ。 出土した[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が[[椿井大塚山古墳]]のものと同笵関係（同一の鋳型または原型を使用して鋳造したもの）にあると見られ、[[椿井大塚山古墳]]の[[被葬者]]の支配下にあった者の墓ではないかと言われている。現在は竹やぶの土入れにより前方部の大半が失われている。 墳頂には8[[メートル|m]]四方の[[埴輪]]をめぐらせた区画があり、その真下に石室がある。[[土師器]]の供献場がある。 == 規模 == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] [[ファイル:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪}}{{small|[[京都市考古資料館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] *全長 - 98m *後円部高さ - 18m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf 南孝雄「寺戸大塚古墳 -向日丘陵の古墳時代前期の首長墓-」] *後円部直径 - 57m == 埋葬施設 == 前方部に長さ 5.3 m、幅約 0.9 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 後円部に長さ 6.45 m、幅約 0.8 m、高さ 1.6 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 == 副葬品 == *後円部 **三角縁神獣鏡 **[[合子]] **[[勾玉]] **石釧 **管玉 *前方部 **浮彫式獣帯鏡 **三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡 **方格規矩四神鏡 **琴柱形石製品 **[[管玉]] **[[刀剣]] **[[槍]] **銅[[鏃]] **[[鉄鏃]] **鉄[[斧]] **鉄[[鎌]] 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡
{{small|後円部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡片
{{small|後円部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|方格規矩四神鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|浮彫式獣帯鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪
{{small|向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 寺戸大塚古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 寺戸大塚古墳自体は、2015年（[[平成]]27年）に「寺戸大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定平成27年（2015年）3月10日[[文部科学省]][[告示]]第38号。 *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「寺戸大塚古墳」の3件を統合、これに他の8基の古墳を追加指定のうえ、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年（2016年）3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（[[文化庁]]記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} {{osm box|n|2144126959}} *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿地方の史跡一覧]] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:てらとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:向日市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{Japanese Kofun | Name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | Location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=Location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * **Front section**: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * **Rear section**: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * **Rear section**: * {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} * {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|合子}} * {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} * Stone bracelets * Tubular beads * **Front section**: * Relief-style animal belt mirrors * Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors * Square-frame patterned mirrors * Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts * Tubular beads * {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} * Spears * Bronze arrowheads * Iron arrowheads * Iron axes * Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * **Terado Ōtsuka Kofun**: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * **Otokuni Kofun Cluster**: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|'''寺戸大塚古墳'''}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m ... 5 KB (569 words) - 20:20, 15 January 2025 * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|'''寺戸大塚古墳'''}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Length 95 m ... 9 KB (1,131 words) - 20:20, 15 January 2025 * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|'''寺戸大塚古墳'''}} ... 8 KB (1,034 words) - 01:17, 15 January 2025 * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ...]]|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:'''寺戸大塚古墳'''|'''寺戸大塚古墳''']]}}, {{nihongo|[[Itsukahara Kofun]]|[[:ja:五塚原古墳|五塚原古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Moto ... 63 KB (7,130 words) - 18:55, 10 January 2025 [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]]

Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=Location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * **Front section**: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * **Rear section**: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * **Rear section**: * {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} * {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|合子}} * {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} * Stone bracelets * Tubular beads * **Front section**: * Relief-style animal belt mirrors * Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors * Square-frame patterned mirrors * Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts * Tubular beads * {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} * Spears * Bronze arrowheads * Iron arrowheads * Iron axes * Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * **Terado Ōtsuka Kofun**: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * **Otokuni Kofun Cluster**: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m ... 5 KB (569 words) - 20:20, 15 January 2025 * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Length 95 m ... 9 KB (1,131 words) - 20:20, 15 January 2025 * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,034 words) - 01:17, 15 January 2025 * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ...]]|[[:ja:天皇の杜古墳|天皇の杜古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:寺戸大塚古墳|寺戸大塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Itsukahara Kofun]]|[[:ja:五塚原古墳|五塚原古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Moto ... 63 KB (7,130 words) - 18:55, 10 January 2025 [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]]

It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * **Front section**: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * **Rear section**: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * **Rear section**: * {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} * {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|合子}} * {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} * Stone bracelets * Tubular beads * **Front section**: * Relief-style animal belt mirrors * Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors * Square-frame patterned mirrors * Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts * Tubular beads * {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} * Spears * Bronze arrowheads * Iron arrowheads * Iron axes * Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * **Terado Ōtsuka Kofun**: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * **Otokuni Kofun Cluster**: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * **Terado Ōtsuka Kofun**: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * **Otokuni Kofun Cluster**: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

Rear Round Section | Location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=Location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * **Front section**: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * **Rear section**: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * **Rear section**: * {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} * {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} * {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} * Stone bracelets * Tubular beads * **Front section**: * Relief-style animal belt mirrors * Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors * Square-frame patterned mirrors * Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts * Tubular beads * {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} * Spears * Bronze arrowheads * Iron arrowheads * Iron axes * Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa

Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=Location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * '''Front section''': A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * '''Rear section''': A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''': Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * '''Otokuni Kofun Cluster''': Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * '''Front section''': A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * '''Rear section''': A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ''' {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ''' {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Stone bracelets ''' Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ''' Relief-style animal belt mirrors ''' Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ''' Square-frame patterned mirrors ''' Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ''' Tubular beads ''' {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ''' Spears ''' Bronze arrowheads ''' Iron arrowheads ''' Iron axes ''' Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''': Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * '''Otokuni Kofun Cluster''': Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun | Name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | Location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=Location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * '''Front section''': A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * '''Rear section''': A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ''' {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ''' {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Stone bracelets ''' Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ''' Relief-style animal belt mirrors ''' Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ''' Square-frame patterned mirrors ''' Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ''' Tubular beads ''' {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ''' Spears ''' Bronze arrowheads ''' Iron arrowheads ''' Iron axes ''' Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''': Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * '''Otokuni Kofun Cluster''': Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''': Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * '''Otokuni Kofun Cluster''': Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * '''Front section''': A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * '''Rear section''': A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ''' {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ''' {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Stone bracelets ''' Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ''' Relief-style animal belt mirrors ''' Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ''' Square-frame patterned mirrors ''' Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ''' Tubular beads ''' {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ''' Spears ''' Bronze arrowheads ''' Iron arrowheads ''' Iron axes ''' Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''': Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * '''Otokuni Kofun Cluster''': Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * '''Front section''': A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * '''Rear section''': A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ''' {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ''' {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Stone bracelets ''' Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ''' Relief-style animal belt mirrors ''' Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ''' Square-frame patterned mirrors ''' Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ''' Tubular beads ''' {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ''' Spears ''' Bronze arrowheads ''' Iron arrowheads ''' Iron axes ''' Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''': Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * '''Otokuni Kofun Cluster''': Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * '''Front section''': A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * '''Rear section''': A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ''' {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ''' {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Stone bracelets ''' Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ''' Relief-style animal belt mirrors ''' Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ''' Square-frame patterned mirrors ''' Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ''' Tubular beads ''' {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ''' Spears ''' Bronze arrowheads ''' Iron arrowheads ''' Iron axes ''' Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror

{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''': Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * '''Otokuni Kofun Cluster''': Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} **Terado Ōtsuka Kofun** (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * **Front section**: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * **Rear section**: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * **Rear section**: ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * **Front section**: ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror

{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror

{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==
