{{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror

{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror

of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror

of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, {{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] ==References==

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Buried Individual = Unknown | Artifacts = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-Shaped Burial Mound)|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the burial mounds in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square platform surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == Dimensions == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == Burial Facilities == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == Grave Goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone artifacts ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic Site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional burial mounds, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == Related Topics == *
