Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | Period = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | Designation = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror

{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror

{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 6]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Teradootsuka Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] [[sn:Teradootsuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Teradootsuka Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] [[sn:Teradootsuka Kofun]]

Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror

{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
