Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Teradootsuka Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa

Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Teradootsuka Kofun]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror

{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror

Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror

Rear Round Section | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun (Keyhole-shaped Kofun)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Muko City|ja|向日市|12=draft}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Swords|ja|刀剣|12=draft}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * Kofun]] * [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=寺戸大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
後円部 |所在地=[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |位置={{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=寺戸大塚
古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長98[[メートル|m]] 高さ9m |築造年代=4世紀 |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者= |出土品=和製[[銅鏡]]・銅[[鏃]]
中国製銅鏡 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]]「寺戸大塚古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） }} '''寺戸大塚古墳'''（てらどおおつかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 向日丘陵古墳群中の1基。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期中葉とみられている。やや西に傾いており、前方部、後円部ともに[[竪穴式石室]]をもつ。 出土した[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が[[椿井大塚山古墳]]のものと同笵関係（同一の鋳型または原型を使用して鋳造したもの）にあると見られ、[[椿井大塚山古墳]]の[[被葬者]]の支配下にあった者の墓ではないかと言われている。現在は竹やぶの土入れにより前方部の大半が失われている。 墳頂には8[[メートル|m]]四方の[[埴輪]]をめぐらせた区画があり、その真下に石室がある。[[土師器]]の供献場がある。 == 規模 == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] [[ファイル:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪}}{{small|[[京都市考古資料館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] *全長 - 98m *後円部高さ - 18m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf 南孝雄「寺戸大塚古墳 -向日丘陵の古墳時代前期の首長墓-」] *後円部直径 - 57m == 埋葬施設 == 前方部に長さ 5.3 m、幅約 0.9 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 後円部に長さ 6.45 m、幅約 0.8 m、高さ 1.6 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 == 副葬品 == *後円部 **三角縁神獣鏡 **[[合子]] **[[勾玉]] **石釧 **管玉 *前方部 **浮彫式獣帯鏡 **三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡 **方格規矩四神鏡 **琴柱形石製品 **[[管玉]] **[[刀剣]] **[[槍]] **銅[[鏃]] **[[鉄鏃]] **鉄[[斧]] **鉄[[鎌]] 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡
{{small|後円部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡片
{{small|後円部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|方格規矩四神鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|浮彫式獣帯鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪
{{small|向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 寺戸大塚古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 寺戸大塚古墳自体は、2015年（[[平成]]27年）に「寺戸大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定平成27年（2015年）3月10日[[文部科学省]][[告示]]第38号。 *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「寺戸大塚古墳」の3件を統合、これに他の8基の古墳を追加指定のうえ、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年（2016年）3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。[https://web.archive.org/web/20160512004751/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（[[文化庁]]記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} {{osm box|n|2144126959}} *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿地方の史跡一覧]] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:てらとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:向日市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{small|向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 寺戸大塚古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 寺戸大塚古墳自体は、2015年（[[平成]]27年）に「寺戸大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定平成27年（2015年）3月10日[[文部科学省]][[告示]]第38号。 *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「寺戸大塚古墳」の3件を統合、これに他の8基の古墳を追加指定のうえ、史跡指定名称を「乙訓古墳群」に変更平成28年（2016年）3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。[https://web.archive.org/web/20160512004751/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html 「史跡等の指定等について」]（[[文化庁]]記者発表、2015年11月20日）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} {{osm box|n|2144126959}} *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿地方の史跡一覧]] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:てらとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:向日市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[寺戸大塚古墳]] to [[Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=寺戸大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
後円部 |所在地=[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |位置={{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|scale:10000}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|label=寺戸大塚
古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長98[[メートル|m]] 高さ9m |築造年代=4世紀 |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者= |出土品=和製[[銅鏡]]・銅[[鏃]]
中国製銅鏡 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]]「寺戸大塚古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） }} '''寺戸大塚古墳'''（てらどおおつかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]]寺戸町芝山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 向日丘陵古墳群中の1基。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期中葉とみられている。やや西に傾いており、前方部、後円部ともに[[竪穴式石室]]をもつ。 出土した[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が[[椿井大塚山古墳]]のものと同笵関係（同一の鋳型または原型を使用して鋳造したもの）にあると見られ、[[椿井大塚山古墳]]の[[被葬者]]の支配下にあった者の墓ではないかと言われている。現在は竹やぶの土入れにより前方部の大半が失われている。 墳頂には8[[メートル|m]]四方の[[埴輪]]をめぐらせた区画があり、その真下に石室がある。[[土師器]]の供献場がある。 == 規模 == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] [[ファイル:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪}}{{small|[[京都市考古資料館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] *全長 - 98m *後円部高さ - 18m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf 南孝雄「寺戸大塚古墳 -向日丘陵の古墳時代前期の首長墓-」] *後円部直径 - 57m == 埋葬施設 == 前方部に長さ 5.3 m、幅約 0.9 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 後円部に長さ 6.45 m、幅約 0.8 m、高さ 1.6 m の竪穴式石室をもつ。 == 副葬品 == *後円部 **三角縁神獣鏡 **[[合子]] **[[勾玉]] **石釧 **管玉 *前方部 **浮彫式獣帯鏡 **三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡 **方格規矩四神鏡 **琴柱形石製品 **[[管玉]] **[[刀剣]] **[[槍]] **銅[[鏃]] **[[鉄鏃]] **鉄[[斧]] **鉄[[鎌]] 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡
{{small|後円部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡片
{{small|後円部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|方格規矩四神鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|浮彫式獣帯鏡
{{small|前方部竪穴式石槨出土。向日市文化資料館企画展示時に撮影。}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Mukō|Muko City]], Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]], bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Mukō|Muko City]], Terado-cho Shibayama, [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]], suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi (lacquerware container)|qq=}} ** [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** [[:en:Sword|Swords]] ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|qq=}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Chohoujiminamihara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|version=104541510|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mukō City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] [[sn:Teradootsuka Kofun]]

Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]], bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Mukō|Muko City]], Terado-cho Shibayama, [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]], suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi (lacquerware container)|qq=}} ** [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** [[:en:Sword|Swords]] ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|qq=}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Chohoujiminamihara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|version=104541510|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi (lacquerware container)|qq=}} ** [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** [[:en:Sword|Swords]] ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|280px]]
Rear Round Section | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Mukō|Muko City]], Terado-cho Shibayama | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E|map_size:10000}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]], bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Mukō|Muko City]], Terado-cho Shibayama, [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]], suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi (lacquerware container)|qq=}} ** [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** [[:en:Sword|Swords]] ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|version=104541510|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and
