revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Terado Otsuka Kofun]][[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Terado Otsuka Kofun]][[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Terado Otsuka Kofun]][[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = File:TeradoOtsukaKofun nt 1.JPG | location = {{ill|Muko|WD=Q850640|1=Mukō}} | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.955833333333|N|135.68861111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Mukō|en|Mukō|WD=Q850640|lt=Muko City|1=Mukō}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|en|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|WD=Q11540994|lt=Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|1=Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi (lacquerware container)|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=Magatama|1=Magatama}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Sword|en|Sword|WD=Q12791|lt=Swords|1=Sword}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|1=Q11638571|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638571}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|version=104541510|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] {{Commons category|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} [[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11457885}} [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|1=Q11638571|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638571}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|version=104541510|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] {{Commons category|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} [[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11457885}} [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Terado Otsuka Kofun]][[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Terado Otsuka Kofun]][[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] | name = Terado Ōtsuka Kofun | image = File:TeradoOtsukaKofun nt 1.JPG | location = {{ill|Muko|WD=Q850640|1=Mukō}} | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.955833333333|N|135.68861111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=57|coordinates=21.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=19.0|map_dot_label=Terado Ōtsuka
Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=location within Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} | type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Total length: 98 m; Height: 9 m | built = 4th century | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Japanese-made {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, bronze arrowheads, Chinese bronze mirror | designation1 = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} ("Terado Ōtsuka Kofun" within Otokuni Kofun Cluster) | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} }} '''Terado Ōtsuka Kofun''' (寺戸大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Mukō|en|Mukō|WD=Q850640|lt=Muko City|1=Mukō}}, Terado-cho Shibayama, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} and is Historic site as a National Historic site. == Overview == The Terado Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the Kofuns in the Mukō Hills Kofun Cluster. It dates back to the middle of the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Both the round rear section and the rectangular front section feature {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as a {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} found in the mound are believed to have been cast using the same mold as those from {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|en|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|WD=Q11540994|lt=Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|1=Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun}}, suggesting that the buried individual may have been under the control of the leader buried in Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun. Currently, much of the front section is eroded due to bamboo grove activity. The mound's top has an 8 m square plattype surrounded by haniwa (clay cylinders), with the main chamber located directly below. A ceremonial area for haniwa offerings is also present. == area == [[File:Terado Otsuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|Top of the rear round section}}]] [[File:寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪 (京都市考古資料館展示).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Cylindrical Haniwa}}{{small|Photographed during a Kyoto City Archaeological Museum exhibition.}}]] * Total length: 98 m * Rear section height: 18 m[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Takao Minami, "Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"] * Rear section diameter: 57 m == burial == * Front section: A 5.3 m long, approximately 0.9 m wide pit-style stone chamber. * Rear section: A 6.45 m long, 0.8 m wide, and 1.6 m tall pit-style stone chamber. == burial goods == * '''Rear section''': ** {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjukyo (triangular-rim deity and beast mirror)|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} ** {{ill|Koshi (lacquerware container)|ja|香合|fr|Kōgō (boîte à encens)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi (lacquerware container)|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=Magatama|1=Magatama}} ** Stone bracelets ** Tubular beads * '''Front section''': ** Relief-style animal belt mirrors ** Triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirrors ** Square-frame patterned mirrors ** Koto-bridge-shaped stone burial goods ** Tubular beads ** {{ill|Sword|en|Sword|WD=Q12791|lt=Swords|1=Sword}} ** Spears ** Bronze arrowheads ** Iron arrowheads ** Iron axes ** Iron sickles 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁櫛歯文帯三仏三獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen comb-tooth-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 後円部竪穴式石槨出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Fragment of triangular-rim vine-pattern mirror
{{small|Excavated from the rear section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 仿製三角縁三神三獣獣帯鏡.JPG|Replica triangular-rim deity-and-beast mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|Square-frame patterned mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳 前方部竪穴式石槨出土 浮彫式獣帯鏡.JPG|Relief-style animal belt mirror
{{small|Excavated from the front section. Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} 寺戸大塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa and morning glory-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a Mukō City Cultural Museum exhibition.}} == designation1 == === National Historic site === * Terado Ōtsuka Kofun: Designated a National Historic site in 2015 as part of the Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 38, March 10, 2015 * Otokuni Kofun Cluster: Expanded in 2016 to include additional Kofuns, with the name changed to Otokuni Kofun Cluster.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2015112003.html "Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc."] (Agency for Cultural Affairs, November 20, 2015) == See Also == * {{Commonscat|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|ja|近畿地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic sites in the Kinki Region|1=Q11638571|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638571}} * [[Motoinari Kofun]] * [[Itsukahara Kofun]] * [[Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun]] * [[List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto)]] ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|version=104541510|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E5%AF%BA%E6%88%B8%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%27%27%27]] {{Commons category|Terado Otsuka Kofun}} [[en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11457885}} [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]
